title: Evaluate upgrading to WiX v6 and the Open Source Maintenance Fee
body:
### Feature request description

[WiX](https://github.com/wixtoolset/wix), the toolset we use to build the Windows installer, has recently changed its [licensing model](https://github.com/wixtoolset/issues/issues/8974), which is effective starting with WiX v6 ([released in April 2025](https://www.firegiant.com/blog/2025/4/7/wix-v600-available/)). The new [Open Source Maintenance Fee](https://opensourcemaintenancefee.org/) requires organizations that use WiX to become a sponsor.

This issue is to evaluate how we can move forward, and in particular:
- Look at becoming a sponsor (CNCF or Red Hat)
- Look at the alternatives (adoption, maturity, activity, licensing, features): [NSIS](https://nsis.sourceforge.io/Main_Page), [Inno Setup](https://jrsoftware.org/isinfo.php), [Squirrel.Windows](https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Windows)...

comments.body:
https://github.com/wixtoolset/wix/blob/main/OSMFEULA.txt
  
> 4. Conflicts with OSI License

> To the extent any term of this Agreement conflicts with User's rights
under the OSI License regarding the Software, the OSI License shall govern.
This Agreement applies only to the Binary Release and does not limit User's
ability to access, modify, or distribute the Software's source code or
self-compiled binaries.

Do we use their binaries? Looking at automation_images install it has does `dotnet.exe tool install --global wix`
https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/d1647d619a3d88f45ed506dd0e5af770398a34ad/win_images/win_packaging.ps1#L26

So where does the installed binary comes from, to me it reads like they only charge for the binaries and active support? But of course IANAL.

You are right. We are currently downloading the binaries, and building the source code would be another option to avoid the fee. But didn't mention it because [from the Q/A maintainers made it clear that this is not welcome](https://opensourcemaintenancefee.org/consumers/faq/#q-what-if-i-dont-want-to-pay-the-maintenance-fee):

> Also, if you choose to not pay the Maintenance Fee, but find yourself returning to check on the status of issues or review answers to questions others ask, you are still using the project and should pay the Maintenance Fee.

The information in the GitHub issues and discussions is a unique source of knowledge, and coding and testing the wxs without access to it would be challenging.

> Also, if you choose to not pay the Maintenance Fee, but find yourself returning to check on the status of issues or review answers to questions others ask, you are still using the project and should pay the Maintenance Fee.

that seems to be their opinion/desire, not part of licensing terms (as long as source code remains under free license at least and issues remain at github) (as I understand it, I am not a lawyer, I may be wrong)

> Do not reference packages via a package manager.

this also seems misleading - if package manager in use is using open source code, not proprietary licensed builds then it is legal to do this (as I understand it, I am not a lawyer, I may be wrong)

(yes, I am aware of lack of funding for OS projects, also from rich companies benefiting from them)
